text: # Strathmore University Foundation ## Transforming Lives Through Education *Empowering future African leaders one scholarship at a time* [Donate Now](https://foundation.strathmore.edu/donate/) ## Our Values ### Excellence SUF is committed to achieving the highest fundraising standards and ensuring effective and impactful resource generation for our University's growth and development.

### Collaboration We prioritize collaboration and partnerships within the university community and beyond, fostering a collective effort to support education, research, and advancement. ### Sustainability We focus on building a sustainable foundation for the University's financial future, ensuring ongoing support for future generations. ### Integrity We uphold honesty, ethics, and transparency in all our actions and interactions.

The foundation operates with the utmost integrity, building trust among stakeholders and maintaining strong fundraising morals. ### Innovation SUF seeks to fundraise for projects, initiatives, and programs that challenge the status quo, drive innovation, and contribute to advancing knowledge and society. ## Your Support Changes Lives At Strathmore University Foundation (SUF), we believe in the power of education to transform lives and create lasting change.

By supporting our mission, you're investing in the leaders of tomorrow who will drive innovation, solve global challenges, and uplift communities across Africa. Join us in bridging the gap between dreams and opportunities. ## Featured Stories ### Dr. Deepak Bedi "When my wife and I learned of Macheo, we knew at once that we had to play a role in nourishing it.

We are hoping to bring this program to the attention of Strathmore Alumni in the USA and have a meet-up in the near future so as to raise significant funds for Macheo." ### John Kevin Owuor John did not have school fees, accommodation or upkeep money to work with, but he had a desire, a smart mind and hope. With support from his family and spiritual leaders, he was driven to start on a blind journey that has proven to be one of the best choices he ever made.

### Hilda Moraa Hilda is a BBIT Graduate Who Sold Her Fourth Year Project Idea and Startup for $1.7 million. The Obama Foundation fellow spent most of her time building a company known as Weza Tele, a mobile solution firm in the distribution, supply chain and financial services.

## Transforming Lives Through Education - **Alumni (2005 to date)**: 15,000+ - **Active Donors**: 1,000+ - **Professional Course Alumni Since Strathmore College**: 30,000+ - **Current Students Supported By Financial Aid**: [Number not specified] ## About Us ### Who We Are Strathmore University Foundation supports Strathmore University and is registered as a Kenyan-based not-for-profit company limited by guarantee.

The sole purpose of the foundation is to mobilize resources to support the University's initiatives such as scholarships, endowments, expansion of the University, and the establishment of Research Centers & Professorial Chairs among other priorities. The Strathmore University Foundation currently administers 10 endowment funds which earn a cumulative annual interest of KES 11.6 million. At the moment these endowment funds are utilized to support scholarships for needy students.

### Our Vision Our vision is to empower Africa with transformative education for lasting change through fundraising for Strathmore University. ### Our Mission Our mission is to empower Africa's leaders through transformative education, fostering innovation, strategic collaboration, and ethical resource mobilization for Strathmore University.

### Our Pillars #### Enhancing Sustainable Fundraising We will strengthen our fundraising efforts by aligning with ESG principles, leveraging the Strathmore University brand, and engaging stakeholders. #### Strengthening Donor Relationships We will build strong relationships with donors by acquiring, cultivating, retaining, and stewarding them.
#### Expanding Donor Base and Reach We will broaden our donor base by targeting emerging segments, sensitizing students to the importance of philanthropy, and fundraising globally.

## Board of Directors - Mr. Zeph Mbugua - Prof. Ruth Kiraka - Dr. Vincent Ogutu - Dr. Majid Twahir - Mrs. Bernadette Musundi - Dr. Edward Mungai - Mr. Anthony Kahindi ## Meet the Staff - **Monica Mwende Mwangi** - DEVELOPMENT DIRECTOR - **Rita Ruigi** - SENIOR DEVELOPMENT MANAGER - **Antony Hayanga** - DEVELOPMENT MANAGER - **Sylvester Orina** - DEVELOPMENT COORDINATOR - **Boniface Mokaya** - INTERN - **Edwin Rimiru** - INTERN - **Medusi Amina** - INTERN - **Mercy Bitutu** - INTERN ## Our Focus

### 01 - Scholarships At SUF, we believe that financial constraints should never hinder a student's potential. Our scholarships ensure that bright, deserving students can access quality education regardless of their financial background. Currently, one in every five students benefits from our financial aid programs. **How You Can Help:** - Sponsor a student with a gift of KES 2,000,000 ($20,000) to establish a named scholarship.

- Join our "Shares for Charity" initiative to make a difference [Sponsor A Student](https://foundation.strathmore.edu/sponsor-a-student/) [Shares for Charity](https://foundation.strathmore.edu/shares-for-charity/) ### 02 - Build a Legacy with Endowments Our endowment funds are a cornerstone of sustainability at Strathmore University. These funds generate consistent support for scholarships, research, and community development.

By contributing to our endowments, you ensure a lasting impact for generations to come. A gift of KES 12 Million ($100,000) will establish a named endowed fund that will exist in perpetuity to provide financial aid to a qualifying undergraduate student every four years. **Key Benefits:** - Guaranteed financial aid for students. - Support for groundbreaking research initiatives. - Tax-deductible contributions for Kenyan organizations.

[Start Your Endowment Today](https://foundation.strathmore.edu/start-your-endowment/) [See Our Impact Stories](https://foundation.strathmore.edu/impact-stories/) ## Impact Projects ### Eng. Kevin O'Byrne Initiative This Project aspires well educated and skilled citizens, underpinned by science, technology and innovation.

[Read More](https://foundation.strathmore.edu/kevin-obyrne-initiative/) ### The STEM Project For transformative, inclusive STEM education that equips the next generation with the skills and opportunities to innovate and lead. [Read More](https://foundation.strathmore.edu/stem-project/) ### Developing African Engineers Over 2.5 million more African engineers are needed in sub-Saharan Africa to tackle its development challenges.

[Read More](https://foundation.strathmore.edu/developing-african-engineers/) ## Why The STEM Project? - The STEM Complex will be a green building providing facilities for Education, Research and Innovation. - Provide students with access to cutting-edge tools, interactive classrooms and specialised laboratories. - Faculty growth, empowerment and exposure to global research collaborations.

- Increased grants and consultancy projects that will be a source of knowledge development, provide opportunities for Innovation and Industry Meeting. ## Engineer Kevin O'Byrne's Legacy The sustainability STEM complex will be dedicated to the late Engineer Kevin O'Byrne, a pioneer teacher at Strathmore College in the 1960s. Among other notable achievements, on graduation, Kevin was the top engineering student in all Universities in Ireland.

He was the structural engineer for some of the iconic buildings that still mark the Nairobi skyline in Kenya. He was an ardent rugby player and the founder member of the Bandits rugby club, Nairobi. The late Eng. O'Byrne was a graduate of the University of Galway (formerly, the National University of Ireland, Galway), which has adopted the CLL and participates in STARS.

The Sustainability STEM complex is the first major step for Strathmore University towards participating in STARS, which will involve mainstreaming sustainability in the University activities. The existing STEM and SDG initiatives at Strathmore University have evolved independently.

Based on experience from other institutions, the University can have more impact by adopting the concept of a Campus as a Learning Lab (CLL) and using "its campus as a lab to test and model innovative sustainability practices, leveraging its research, academic and operations functions."

The University of British Columbia, Canada adopted the CLL concept at a University level thus "integrating academic research and teaching with campus planning, infrastructure, operations and community development". Princeton University has a CLL website where academic research opportunities are listed, projects are archived, and sustainability efforts are noted. The Sustainability STEM complex building and facilities are estimated to cost USD/EUR 20 million.

Strathmore University has committed to fund 20% of the project costs and seeks partners for the remaining amount. Whereas upfront donations are preferred, the University is open to considering donations spread through the sustainability STEM complex construction period of 3 years. Donations to Strathmore University Foundation by organizations in Kenya are allowable as tax-deductible expenses in calculating the taxable profit of such organizations.

[Donate Now](https://foundation.strathmore.edu/donate/) ## Partnership Options ### Diamond Partner **Ksh 80 million** **Available Spaces**: Auditorium, Incubation, and Innovation Centre [Donate](https://foundation.strathmore.edu/donate/) ### Platinum Partner **Ksh 60 million** **Available Spaces**: Specialised Labs, Computing Labs, and Makerspaces [Donate](https://foundation.strathmore.edu/donate/) ### Gold Partner **Ksh 40 million** **Available Spaces**: Conference Room

[Donate](https://foundation.strathmore.edu/donate/) ### Silver Partner **Ksh 20 million** **Available Spaces**: Lecture Theatres, Elec Lab, Civil Lab, and Mech Lab [Donate](https://foundation.strathmore.edu/donate/) ### Bronze Partner **Ksh 10 million** **Available Spaces**: Meeting Rooms and Huddle Rooms [Donate](https://foundation.strathmore.edu/donate/) ## Research & Academic Centers *February, 2025* The creation of an endowed academic Centre allows the university to develop and enhance its

research and learning in a priority area of study and to recruit faculty members from among the best scholars in the world. This is vital to Strathmore's growth and excellence. The University, however, cannot rely on tuition fees alone to attract the world leading professors. Strathmore seeks to engage with its industry partners and friends who share the same vision of maintaining academic excellence. Industry has also the need to grow solutions in quasi-incubated spaces.

These spaces can be provided effectively by academia where research take precedence and the teams within the university can offer input to solutions that industry seeks. Funding a Centre also allows the university to dedicate funds to providing world class learning facilities that provide students with an excellent learning environment.

The Centre team appointment is one of distinction, made to advance the academic programs of the University and to enhance teaching and research in an area within the university. There are various benefits that our partners will accrue for having sponsored the Centre. These include: a) Priority to choose the naming of the Centre. b) Involvement in selection of the Professor/s who will head the Centre c) Brand publicity d) Involvement in the selection of the Chairs in the Professorial Chairs.

Please get in touch with us if you are interested in sponsoring a Center at Strathmore University. ## Contact Information **Strathmore Foundation** Address: Ole Sangale Road, off Langata Road, Madaraka Estate, Nairobi, Kenya.

Location: Strathmore University, STC Building, 3rd floor Email: suf@strathmore.edu Phone: (254) 703 034290 **Office Hours** Mon – Fri: 8 am- 5 pm Weekends: Closed ## Clubs in Strathmore - French club - German Club - Hospitality and Tourism Students Society - HATTS - Japanese Club - KMUN - Mental Health Club - Presidential Awards Club - Readers and Writers Club - SASS - Spanish Club - Strathmore Business Club - Strathmore Envronmental and Sustainability Club - Strathmore Marketing Club -
